detectors.description:

	But does not have a function to withdraw the ether

	But does not have a function to withdraw the ether

detectors.description:
Reentrancy in Diwali._transfer(address,address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#1829-2043):
	External calls:
	- swapBack() (Diwali.sol#1961)
		- uniswapV2Router.swapExactTokensForTokensSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens(tokenAmount,0,path,treasuryWallet,block.timestamp) (Diwali.sol#2065-2077)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- super._transfer(from,address(this),fees) (Diwali.sol#2023)
		- _balances[sender] = senderBalance - amount (Diwali.sol#755)
		- _balances[recipient] += amount (Diwali.sol#759)
	ERC20._balances (Diwali.sol#441) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- ERC20._mint(address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#791-811)
	- ERC20._transfer(address,address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#729-769)
	- ERC20.balanceOf(address) (Diwali.sol#571-575)
	- super._transfer(address(this),uniswapV2Pair,tokensForLiquidity) (Diwali.sol#2029)
		- _balances[sender] = senderBalance - amount (Diwali.sol#755)
		- _balances[recipient] += amount (Diwali.sol#759)
	ERC20._balances (Diwali.sol#441) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- ERC20._mint(address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#791-811)
	- ERC20._transfer(address,address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#729-769)
	- ERC20.balanceOf(address) (Diwali.sol#571-575)
	- super._transfer(from,to,amount) (Diwali.sol#2041)
		- _balances[sender] = senderBalance - amount (Diwali.sol#755)
		- _balances[recipient] += amount (Diwali.sol#759)
	ERC20._balances (Diwali.sol#441) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- ERC20._mint(address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#791-811)
	- ERC20._transfer(address,address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#729-769)
	- ERC20.balanceOf(address) (Diwali.sol#571-575)
	- swapping = false (Diwali.sol#1965)
	Diwali.swapping (Diwali.sol#1451) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- Diwali._transfer(address,address,uint256) (Diwali.sol#1829-2043)

	- sellTotalFees = sellTreasuryFee + sellLiquidityFee (Diwali.sol#1785)
